Burrow.description: Speed Skill - How quickly the Pokémon can move underground. Value = spaces/meters per round underground. While burrowing, knows surroundings up to X meters away. Learning Dig grants Burrow 3 or +3 if already has it.

Intelligence.description: Defines the Pokémon's competence. 1=Feeble-minded, 2=Self-aware, 3=Can follow lead, 4=Can build/use tools, 5=Human intellect, 6=Can lead/act alone, 7=Genius/speaks human languages.

Jump.description: Max jump height in a single bound. 1=3ft/1m, 2=6ft/1.8m, 3=10ft/3m, 4=15ft/4.5m, 5=20ft/6m, 6=25ft/7.6m, 7=35ft/10.6m, 8=50ft/15.2m, 9=70ft/21m, 10=100ft/30.5m. Learning Bounce raises Jump +1.

Overland.description: Speed Skill - How quickly the Pokémon moves on dry land. Value = spaces/meters per round on normal terrain.

Power.description: Pokémon's strength/lifting capacity. 1=10lbs/5kg, 2=50lbs/23kg, 3=100lbs/45kg, 4=200lbs/90kg, 5=350lbs/158kg, 6=500lbs/227kg, 7=750lbs/340kg, 8=1000lbs/455kg, 9=2500lbs/1135kg, 10=4000lbs/1815kg. Learning Strength raises Power +1.

Sky.description: Speed Skill - How quickly the Pokémon moves while flying/floating/levitating. Value = spaces/meters per round in air. Can stay airborne 5+ minutes. Learning Fly grants Sky 4 or +4 if already has it.

Surface.description: Speed Skill - How quickly the Pokémon moves on water surface. Value = spaces/meters per round. Learning Surf grants Surface 4 or +4, and removes Sinker skill.

Underwater.description: Speed Skill - How quickly the Pokémon moves underwater. Value = spaces/meters per round. Must surface every 4 rounds unless has Gilled. Learning Dive grants Underwater 3 or +3.

Alluring.description: Smells very pleasant, may attract wild Pokémon. Learning Sweet Scent grants this skill.

Amorphous.description: Has inconsistent shape, can flatten/reform like water, stretch and condense. Can access places others cannot.

Aura.description: Can project thoughts to Pokémon and humans, read auras (not thoughts). Aura shade indicates intentions. Learning Aura Sphere, Dragon/Dark/Heal Pulse, Focus Blast, Force Palm, Detect, or Vacuum Wave grants this.

Blender.description: Advanced Stealth user. Silent while Shifting. Ranged Moves need +2 to Accuracy Check to hit. Cannot be targeted by Ranged Moves through Rough Terrain unless used Ranged Move recently.

Bloom.description: Cherrim transforms in Sunny weather from closed bud to open flower form.

Climber.description: Treats vertical terrain as normal non-hindering terrain (except smooth surfaces) while using Overland.

Dream Smoke.description: Can produce Dream Smoke for Dream Doctors. Learning Dream Eater grants this skill.

Egg Warmer.description: Keeps eggs safely warmer than humans. Eggs hatch 2 hours/day faster per species when watched over.

Family Ties.description: Can naturally make eggs from another species (Nidoran, Jynx/Mime Jr., Miltank/Tauros, Plusle/Minun, Illumise/Volbeat, Lunatone/Solrock).

Firestarter.description: Can produce flames from lighter-sized puffs to tree-engulfing bursts. Learning Ember, Fire Blast, Fire Spin, Flame Wheel, or Flamethrower grants this.

Fountain.description: Can produce freshwater from sprinkles to fire-hose force. Learning Water Gun, Water Pulse, Hydro Pump, or Water Sport grants this.

Fragile.description: Archen/Archeops default Ability is always Defeatist.

Freezer.description: Can turn ground into Icy Terrain (1d20 determines area). Learning Aurora Beam, Blizzard, Ice Beam, or Sheer Cold grants this.

Gilled.description: Can breathe underwater indefinitely, never needs to surface.

Glow.description: Can emit light from body. May attract or ward wild Pokémon. Learning Flash or Tail Glow grants this.

Groundshaper.description: Can transform "terrain": create Rough Terrain or flatten to Basic Terrain in 1m radius. Learning Earthquake or Magnitude grants this.

Guster.description: Can create wind from light breeze to powerful bursts. Learning Gust or Tailwind grants this.

Husk.description: Minior default Ability is always Shields Down.

Icestep.description: Treats Icy or Snowy terrain as Basic Terrain.

Inflatable.description: Can expand to 125% size. -3 to hit while inflated, becomes Blocking Terrain. Learning Growth or Stockpile grants this.

Invisibility.description: Can turn invisible for up to 4 minutes. +4 to Accuracy Checks to hit. Cannot use Moves while invisible.

Juicer.description: "Shuckle": Berry held 24hrs becomes Berry Juice (doubled HP/PP recovery). After 2 weeks, becomes Rare Candy.

Magnetic.description: Can manipulate magnetic fields, attract/repel iron/steel, sense magnetic north. Learning Magnet Rise or Magnet Bomb grants this.

Materializer.description: Can form rock from nothing (pebble to boulder). Costs 2 HP per 5 lbs formed, max 50 lbs per round. Learning Rock Wrecker grants this.

Pack Mon.description: Wild unevolved forms are "Helpful" and will do tasks. Cannot be ordered to attack. Capture attempts fail.

Phasing.description: Can Shift through Hindering/Blocking Terrain. Can turn intangible for up to 30 seconds but cannot use Moves.

Reach.description: Can make Melee attacks from up to 5 meters/spaces away.

Schooling.description: Wishiwashi default Ability is always Schooling.

Shrinkable.description: Can shrink to 25% size. +3 to Accuracy Checks to hit while shrunken. Cannot use Moves. Learning Minimize grants this.

Sinker.description: Cannot swim. Loses 25% max HP per round submerged in deep water. Removed by gaining Surface or Underwater skill.

Soulless.description: "Shedinja": Always has Wonder Guard, Max HP is always 1, never makes Death Saves, cannot have Solid Rock ability.

Sprouter.description: Can rapidly grow plants (1m per 5 sec, up to 125% natural size). Costs 1 HP per 10 sec. Learning Ingrain, Natural Gift, Nature/Secret Power, or Synthesis grants this.

Stance.description: Aegislash default Ability is always Stance Change.

Stealth.description: Silent while Shifting. Cannot be targeted by Ranged Moves through Rough Terrain unless used Ranged Move recently.

Sticky Climber.description: Treats vertical terrain AND ceiling as normal non-hindering terrain while using Overland.

Telekinetic.description: Can move objects with mind (max weight = level × 5 lbs, range = level meters). Learning Psychic or Telekinesis grants this.

Telepath.description: Can read surface thoughts and project thoughts. Cannot read minds 2+ Intelligence ranks different.

Threaded.description: Can use silk/vine to pull self to objects or objects to self (10m range, AC 6 vs creatures). Learning Electro Web, Spider Web, String Shot, Vine/Power Whip grants this.

Tracker.description: Strong sense of smell. Track known "scent": DC 11. Random "scent": DC 16. Specific "scent": DC 20. Once per hour. Learning Odor Sleuth grants this.

Troublesome.description: Slakoth/Slaking default Ability is always Truant.

Weathershape.description: Castform changes form with weather (orange=Sunny, blue-grey=Rain, light blue=Hail).

Weeble.description: "Wobbuffett": Counter/Mirror Coat are EOT instead of Battle frequency. After 1st use each, DEF/SDEF -10 for those moves.

Wimp.description: Wimpod default Ability is always Wimp Out.

Wired.description: Can enter machines and travel through connected electronics. Read machine "data": Daily check. Control "machine": DC 16, once per hour.

Wormadam's Coat.description: Female Burmy gains Grass (greenery), Ground (rocky), or Steel (urban) type on evolution based on surroundings.

Zapper.description: Can produce electricity and send currents through conductors. Recharge "devices": DC 13, Daily. Learning Charge, Discharge, Thunderbolt, or Thundershock grants this.

Ace Trainer.description: Masters of battle strategy who inspire their Pokémon to victory through superior tactics and training.

Breeder.description: Patient caretakers who raise Pokémon with love, specializing in eggs, evolution, and nurturing growth.

Capture Specialist.description: Tech experts who modify Poké Balls and master advanced capture techniques.

Coordinator.description: Performers who showcase Pokémon beauty and skill in contests and performances.

Martial Artist.description: Fighters who train their bodies to battle alongside their Pokémon in combat.

Mystic.description: Those who channel ancient and supernatural powers to bond with Pokémon and the spirit world.

Psychic.description: Individuals with mental powers who use telekinesis, telepathy, and other psionic abilities.

Ranger.description: Nature guardians who protect wild Pokémon and excel in wilderness survival.

Researcher.description: Scholars who study Pokémon behavior, biology, and the mysteries of the world.

Chaser.description: Specializes in pursuing and hunting down specific targets.

Enduring Soul.description: Masters of stamina who outlast opponents in prolonged battles.

Stat Ace.description: Experts at maximizing specific stats for optimal performance.

Strategist.description: Tactical geniuses who plan several moves ahead.

Tag Battler.description: Specialists in double battles and team coordination.

Type Ace.description: Masters of a specific Pokémon type.

Underdog.description: Fighters who excel when facing stronger opponents.

Botanist.description: Specialists in Grass-type Pokémon and plant cultivation.

Chef.description: Masters of Pokémon food and beneficial cooking.

Evolver.description: Experts in triggering and controlling Pokémon evolution.

Groomer.description: Specialists in Pokémon care and appearance.

Hatcher.description: Masters of egg breeding and hatching techniques.

Medic.description: Healers who treat Pokémon injuries and ailments.

Move Tutor.description: Teachers who help Pokémon learn new moves.

Artificer.description: Crafters of specialized tools and devices.

Collector.description: Dedicated to completing collections of rare Pokémon.

Engineer.description: Technical experts who build and modify equipment.

Juggler.description: Masters of handling multiple Poké Balls simultaneously.

Pokéball Designer.description: Creators of custom Poké Ball designs.

Trapper.description: Experts in setting traps for wild Pokémon.

Choreographer.description: Masters of dance and movement routines.

Fashion Designer.description: Creators of stylish Pokémon accessories and outfits.

Athlete.description: Peak physical performers in various sports.

Aura User.description: Warriors who harness the power of Aura.

Dirty Fighter.description: Combat pragmatists who fight without rules.

Yogi.description: Masters of meditation and inner peace.

Bard.description: Musicians who channel power through song and music.

Bodysnatcher.description: Those who can possess or swap bodies.

Conduit.description: Channels for spiritual and elemental energy.

Godspeaker.description: Those who communicate with legendary Pokémon.

Shaman.description: Spirit mediums who commune with the other side.

Air Adept.description: Psychics who control wind and air currents.

Earth Shaker.description: Psychics who manipulate the ground and earth.

Empath.description: Those who sense and influence emotions.

Fire Breather.description: Psychics who create and control flames.

Hex Maniac.description: Dark psychics who specialize in curses.

Rain Waker.description: Psychics who control water and weather.

Coach.description: Trainers who specialize in teaching others.

Commander.description: Leaders who direct Pokémon in tactical formations.

Detective.description: Investigators who solve mysteries with Pokémon.

Rider.description: Experts in mounted Pokémon travel and combat.

Signer.description: Those who form special bonds through marks.

Cryptozoologist.description: Seekers of rare and legendary Pokémon.

Dream Doctor.description: Specialists in dream manipulation and study.

Petrologist.description: Experts in fossils and ancient Pokémon.

Photographer.description: Artists who capture Pokémon through photography.

Watcher.description: Observers who study Pokémon behavior in nature.

Aim for the Horn!.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, +1 use per day
Aim for the Horn!.target: Your Pokémon targeting with a Move
Aim for the Horn!.effect: On a roll of 19 or 20 during Accuracy Check, your Pokémon's attack will deal Neutral damage to a target regardless of Resistances or Immunities.

Aim for the Horn! +.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, +1 use per day
Aim for the Horn! +.target: Your Pokémon targeting with a Move
Aim for the Horn! +.effect: On a roll of 16-20 during Accuracy Check, your Pokémon's attack will deal Neutral damage to a target regardless of Resistances or Immunities. Replaces Aim for the Horn!

Back Off.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
Back Off.trigger: A Trainer, not involved in a Gym or Competition battle, challenges you
Back Off.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is higher than 10, the Trainer is intimidated and withdraws their challenge.

Chosen One.trigger: You fail to roll high enough for any type of check
Chosen One.effect: You don't fail the check. You may not apply this to a Pokémon's check.

Dual Wielding.prerequisites: Level 10, 12 HP, 12 ATK, 12 DEF, 12 SATK, 12 SDEF, 12 SPD
Dual Wielding.effect: You may control 2 Pokémon at the same time. During a round of battle, they are each included in the battle queue. You are still only allotted 1 Trainer Action. Cannot use with Multitasking in the same round.

Give It Your All.frequency: One Time Use Only – +1 use per 10 levels gained
Give It Your All.effect: Target one of your Pokémon's Moves. For one use, it is a Critical Hit that cannot miss.

Hey Guys, Watch This.effect: Instead of gaining a new Feature when you level up, you gain 1 Feat Point. A Feat Point can be spent at any time you can use a Trainer Action (doesn't take an action). When spent, add any Feature whose prerequisites you meet. Cannot regain spent Feat Points.

I Can Take a Hit.effect: When taking damage from anything reduce that damage by 5. Does not reduce the cost of activating Features that require HP loss.

I Can Take a Hit +.effect: When taking damage from anything reduce that damage by 10. Does not reduce the cost of activating Features that require HP loss. Replaces I Can Take a Hit.

I Believe in You!.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
I Believe in You!.effect: For the remainder of combat, your Pokémon deals an additional 1d6 during the Move's damage for each point you have in SDEF modifier.

League Member.effect: Become a Frontier Brain or Gym Leader. Must accept challenges weekly. If you lose, give victor a Medal/Badge. Paid 2000₽/week (Leaders) or 4500₽/week (Brains) if accepting 3+ challenges. Add 2 to your SDEF stat.

Let Me Help You With That.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, +1 use per day
Let Me Help You With That.target: An allied Trainer making a check
Let Me Help You With That.effect: The ally has +2 added to their check.

Let Me Help You With That +.prerequisites: Let Me Help You With That, Level 10
Let Me Help You With That +.frequency: Daily – Every 7 levels gained, +1 use per day
Let Me Help You With That +.target: An allied Trainer making a check
Let Me Help You With That +.effect: The ally has +5 added to their check. Replaces Let Me Help You With That.

Let's Get That Lock Open.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, +1 use per day
Let's Get That Lock Open.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the result is higher than 15, the lock is unlocked and is undamaged and doesn't appear tampered with.

Look Out!.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
Look Out!.effect: During this round, if your targeted Pokémon is attacked, flip a coin until you flip tails. The foe must roll 1 higher during Accuracy Check to hit your Pokémon for each heads.

Mega Evolver.prerequisites: 10 HP, 10 ATK, 10 DEF, 10 SATK, 10 SDEF, 10 SPD, a Key Stone
Mega Evolver.target: A Pokémon with a Mega Stone
Mega Evolver.effect: Mega Evolve the Pokémon for 10 minutes or until it is returned while under 0 HP.

Multitasking.prerequisites: Level 10, 12 HP, 12 ATK, 12 DEF, 12 SATK, 12 SDEF, 12 SPD
Multitasking.effect: During encounters, you may perform 2 different Trainer Actions per round. You may not use more than one Trainer Action related Feature per round. Cannot use with Dual Wielding in the same round.

Not Yet!.trigger: Your Pokémon is lowered to 0 HP or less, but not greater than -100% HP
Not Yet!.effect: Before fainting, the targeted Pokémon can make one last shift and Move and then immediately faints afterwards. Cannot be used with Endeavor, Explosion, Flail, Pain Split, Reversal or Self-destruct.

Random Knowledge.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, +1 use per day
Random Knowledge.target: Anything you have line of sight to, or an idea/phrase you just heard
Random Knowledge.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers. If you roll higher than 14, you know about the thing you targeted. If targeting a Pokémon, you must have already scanned it with a Pokédex.

Remedial First Aid.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
Remedial First Aid.effect: Roll 1d20 and Add your SATK or SDEF Modifier. Heal the target this much HP.

Satoshi's Karma.prerequisites: Satoshi's Luck, released 3 fully-evolved loyal Pokémon
Satoshi's Karma.effect: You may re-roll any single die. Replaces Satoshi's Luck.

Satoshi's Luck.effect: At the cost of 15 HP (cannot be reduced), you may re-roll any single die.

Step Aside!.frequency: One Time Use Only – +1 use per 10 levels gained
Step Aside!.trigger: Your Pokémon is targeted by a Move or Trainer Attack
Step Aside!.effect: Your Pokémon immediately Shifts. If it Shifts away from a targeted area, the Move or Trainer Attack is avoided. If unable to shift out of the way, it takes no damage. Only "Spirit Surge" effects may still activate.

Study Session.prerequisites: Purchased a book at least once
Study Session.effect: Must buy a book for 70₽ at each new town's PokéMart. Your Special Attack OR Special Defense stat gains 1 point. Can take multiple times (still only 70₽ per book).

Voltorb Flip.prerequisites: 12 HP Stat or 12 SATK or 12 SDEF
Voltorb Flip.trigger: A human or Pokémon tries to read your mind
Voltorb Flip.effect: Roll 1d20 + SATK or SDEF modifier. If 13+, you think of Voltorb Flip and block the mind reader. If successful, mind reading attempts fail for the next hour.

Workout.effect: Must exercise at least once every 3 days (30 min, lose 10 HP). Your HP, ATK, DEF, or SPD stat gains 1 point. Can take multiple times (still only lose 10 HP total).

Use Pokédex.target: A Pokémon not yet identified within 10m
Use Pokédex.effect: Point your Pokédex at the unidentified Pokémon to identify it (takes 12 seconds or 2 combat rounds). For every 40 Pokémon identified, your Trainer Levels Up!

Arms User.effect: Your Accuracy Check for Arms attacks is 6. Add highest of ATK or SPD modifiers to damage. "Damage": DB1 (1d10+4), DB2 at Lv7+ (1d12+6), DB3 at Lv15+ (2d8+6).

Weapon of Choice.effect: Choose a specific weapon type (longsword, unarmed, slingshot, etc.). Accuracy Check is 4. "Damage": DB2 (1d12+6), DB4 at Lv7+ (2d10+8), DB6 at Lv15+ (3d10+12). Can take multiple times for different weapons.

Attack Order.description: Attack Order is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Bug Bite.description: If the target has a consumable Held Item, Bug Bite destroys the Item and Bug Bite’s user may use the Item’s effect.

Bug Buzz.description: Bug Buzz creates a 3-meter Burst. Bug Buzz lowers the target’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Defend Order.description: Raise the user’s Defense 1 Combat Stage and raise the user’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Fell Stinger.description: If the user lowers a target to 0 HP or less while using Fell Stinger, raise the user’s Attack 2 Combat Stages.

First Impression.description: If a foe wants to declare a Move, you may instead use First Impression on their turn before they can use their Move. First Impression may not be Interrupted. First Impression may only be used if no other Moves have been performed during this encounter.

Fury Cutter.description: If Fury Cutter is used successfully and consecutively on the same target, use 1d12+6 on the second hit instead, 2d12+12 on the third hit instead, 3d12+18 on the fourth hit instead and 4d12+24 on the fifth hit and any successful hits following the fifth hit instead.

Heal Order.description: The user regains HP equal to half of its full HP.

Infestation.description: The target gains the Coat, Infested. Infested Pokpmon lose 1/8th of their full HP at the beginning of each of their turns for 5 rounds. Do not apply weakness, resistance or stats to the lost HP. Infested Pokpmon are Trapped and cannot move more than 3 meters from the user.

Leech Life.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target.

Lunge.description: Lower the user’s Attack 1 Combat Stage after damage.

Megahorn.description: The target is Pushed 3-meters. The target takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6.

Pin Missile.description: Pin Missile can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Pin Missile attack on that turn.

Pollen Puff.description: If the target is the user’s ally, instead of dealing damage, Pollen Puff heals the target after calculating damage.

Powder.description: The target receives a Powder Coat. If the target uses a Fire-Type Move, the Coat is destroyed and they lose 1/4th of their max HP.

Quiver Dance.description: Raise the user’s Special Attack 1 Combat Stage, raise the user’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage and raise the user’s Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Rage Powder.description: Rage Powder creates a Column 1 meter wide. All legal targets may only target the user when using a Move until the user is knocked out or switched out. This Effect lasts for one round of combat.

Signal Beam.description: Signal Beam creates a Column 1 meter wide. Signal Beam confuses the target and any Legal Targets on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Silver Wind.description: Silver Wind creates a Column that is 2meters wide. If you rolled 11-20 during Accuracy Check for Silver Wind, the user has each of its stats raised 1 Combat Stage. If you rolled 16-20 on the Accuracy Roll, all allies in the Column also have each of their stats raised 1 Combat Stage and are not damaged by Silver Wind.

Steamroller.description: Steamroller Flinches the target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check. If the target is Small, Steamroller deals an additional 1d10.

Sticky Web.description: Set 10 square meters of Sticky Web, all 10 meters must be adjacent with at least one other space of Sticky Web next to each other. Sticky Web cause all foes to have their Speed Combat Stage lowered 1 when moving onto or over the occupied spaces with an Overland Skill. A foe can only lose 1 Combat Stage per turn this way.

String Shot.description: String Shot creates a Column 2-meters wide. All Legal Targets within the Column lower their Speed 1 Combat Stage. If a target is hit by String Shot 5 times within 5 rounds of combat, they are Trapped. If a target’s Speed Combat Stage has already been lowered 6 times, String Shot Traps them.

Struggle Bug.description: Struggle Bug creates a Column 1 meter wide. Struggle Bug lowers all Legal Targets Special Attack 1 Combat Stage. Dark Moves

Tail Glow.description: Raise the user’s Special Attack 3 Combat Stages.

Twineedle.description: Twineedle can hit up to 2 times. If Twineedle misses its first target, you may still use it a second time. If you rolled an 18-20 on either Accuracy Check, the target is Poisoned.

U-Turn.description: If U-Turn successfully hits its target, the user deals damage and then immediately is returned to its Poke Ball in the same turn. A New Pokpmon may immediately be sent out.

Assurance.description: If Assurance’s target has already been damaged by a Move on the same round Assurance is being used, use 4d12+16 instead for Assurance’s Damage Dice Roll.

Beat Up.description: 1d8+X - Battle - 2 Beat Up can hit up to X times. X is the amount of Pokpmon on the user’s roster including the user, up to a maximum of 6. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Beat Up attack on that turn. For each target, use a different Pokpmon’s Attack stat from your roster to calculate damage.

Bite.description: Bite Flinches the target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check.

Crunch.description: Crunch lowers the target’s Defense 1 Combat Stage on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Dark Pulse.description: Dark Pulse creates a 3-meter Burst. Dark Pulse Flinches all Legal Targets on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Dark Void.description: Dark Void creates a 10-meter Burst. All Legal Targets fall Asleep.

Darkest Lariat.description: Dark Lariat ignores the target’s Combat Stages during Accuracy Check and when dealing damage.

Embargo.description: If the target has a held item, they cannot use it for the remainder of the encounter.

Fake Tears.description: Lower the target’s Special Defense 2 Combat Stages.

Flatter.description: Flatter Confuses the target and raises the target’s Special Attack 1 Combat Stage.

Fling.description: The user may throw its held item when using Fling, changing its Damage Dice Roll. If a berry is thrown, the target receives the berry’s benefits as if the target, instead of taking any damage, consumed the berry. If an item that causes an effect is thrown, the item’s effect is activated. If any other item is thrown the user deals an additional 2d6 during Damage Dice Roll. All berries or other consumable held items are destroyed when used with Fling. Items that are not destroyed by Fling may be retrieved after an encounter.

Foul Play.description: The target reveals its Attack stat. When calculating damage, add the target’s Attack stat instead of the user’s Attack stat.

Hone Claws.description: Raise the user’s Attack 1 Combat Stage. For the remainder of Combat, the user’s racy Checks to hit.

Knock Off.description: If the target has a held item, the target drops it and the item cannot be picked up or held for the remainder of combat.

Memento.description: The user’s HP is set to 0. The user does not need a line of sight to the target. Memento cannot be used if the user is at or below 0 HP. Each of the target’s stats are lowered 2 Combat Stages.

Nasty Plot.description: Raise the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages.

Night Daze.description: The Target must roll +1 during Accuracy Checks for the remainder of the encounter on 13-20 during Accuracy Check.

Night Slash.description: Night Slash is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Parting Shot.description: If Parting Shot successfully hits its target, the user lowers the target’s Attack and Special Attack 1 Combat Stage and then immediately is returned to its Poke Ball in the same turn. A New Pokpmon may immediately be sent out.

Payback.description: If the target damaged Payback’s user on their last turn, deals 4d12+16 instead for Damage Dice Roll.

Power Trip.description: For every Combat Stage the user is above 0, per stat, add 1d10 to Power Trip’s Damage Dice Roll.

Punishment.description: Punishment deals an additional 1d10 for each Combat Stage the target has above 0 per raised stat up to an additional 6d10 per stat.

Pursuit.description: If the foe is fleeing or being switched out, Pursuit may be used as an Interrupt. While being used as an Interrupt, Pursuit may shift the user adjacent to the fleeing foe, up to 25 meters, ignoring Speed Skills, and attack them before they escape or are switched out with an additional 3d10+12 added to the Damage Dice Roll.

Quash.description: Quash will not affect a Legal Target if it has already acted that round. Quash causes the target to act as if their Speed is 0, meaning their action is last for that turn.

Snarl.description: Snarl creates a 5-meter Burst. All Legal Targets have their Special Attack lowered 1 Combat Stage.

Snatch.description: If the target is gaining a Combat Stage, you may use Snatch. The user gains the same Combat Stages the target does. Then, the target loses all Combat Stages they gained that turn. If the target is using a No Damage - Self Move, you may use Snatch. You use the No Damage - Self Move and the target does not.

Sucker Punch.description: If something is targeting the user with a damaging Attack - Melee Move, you may use Sucker Punch on the offender as an Interrupt.

Switcheroo.description: The user and the target exchange held items.

Taunt.description: Taunt’s target can only use Moves that have Damage Dice Rolls for the next 1d4+1 turns.

Thief.description: Thief takes the target’s held item and attaches it to Thief’s user, if the user is not holding anything.

Throat Chop.description: On hit, the target may not use Moves with Sound effects for 1d8 rounds.

Topsy-Turvy.description: If the target has any negative Combat Stages, they are now positive. If the target has any positive Combat Stages, they are now negative.

Torment.description: For 1d4+2 turns, Torment’s target may not use a Move they have already used in their last 2 turns. Dragon Moves

Clanging Scales.description: Clanging Scales creates a 3-meter Burst. Lower the user’s Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Core Enforcer.description: Core Enforcer creates a 10-meter Burst. If Core Enforcer’s user has been attacked by any targets hit by Core Enforcer, those targets lose their Abilities for the remainder of the encounter.

Draco Meteor.description: Draco Meteor creates a 4-meter Blast. Lower the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages after damage.

Dragon Breath.description: Dragon Breath creates a Column 1 meter wide. Dragon Breath Paralyzes all Legal Targets on 15-20 during Accuracy Check.

Dragon Dance.description: Raise the user’s Attack 1 Combat Stage and raise the user’s Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Dragon Rage.description: Dragon Rage always deals 40 points of Damage. Do not apply weakness or resistance. Do not apply stats.

Dragon Rush.description: The target is Pushed 3-meters and it takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6. Dragon Rush Flinches the target on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Dragon Tail.description: The target is Pushed 40-meters expelling them from the encounter if the terrain allows. They may still rejoin the encounter if they want to. During a trainer battle, the target’s owner must recall the Pushed target and replace them.

Dragon’s Ascent.description: Lower the target’s Defense and Special Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Dual Chop.description: Dual Chop can hit up to 2 times. If Dual Chop misses its first target, you may still use it a second time.

Outrage.description: Outrage’s user must make another Melee attack for 1d2 more turns. The attack is identical to Outrage except for Outrage’s effect. After the first target is declared, Outrage targets the nearest Legal Target for its second or third round of use if the original target is unconscious.  If potential targets are an equal distance from the user, you may choose the target. After Outrage’s additional use(s) end(s), the user becomes Confused. For Pokpmon users, the additional 1d2 are their Trainer’s Command Actions, unless the Trainer is unconscious, in which case the Pokpmon acts on its own until finished.

Roar of Time.description: Roar of Time creates a 10-meter Burst. All legal targets are Trapped until they roll a 16-20 saving throw. They may attempt to make this save at any time once per round.

Spacial Rend.description: Spacial Rend is a Critical Hit on 11-20 during Accuracy Check.

Twister.description: Twister Flinches the target on 18-20 during Accuracy Check. If the target is airborne as a result of Fly or Sky Drop, Twister can hit them, ignoring Range and deals 2d12+16 for its Damage Dice Roll instead. Electric Moves

Bolt Strike.description: Bolt Strike Paralyzes the target on 1720 during Accuracy Check.

Charge.description: If the user performs an Electric Move on their next turn that deals damage, add its Damage Dice Roll an extra time to the damage. Raise the user’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Charge Beam.description: Charge Beam creates a Column 1 meter wide. Charge Beam raises the user’s Special Attack 1 Combat Stage on 1120 during Accuracy Check.

Discharge.description: Discharge creates a 3-meter Burst. Discharge Paralyzes all legal targets on 1520 during Accuracy Check.

Eerie Impulse.description: The target’s Special Attack is lowered 2 Combat Stages.

Electric Terrain.description: For 5 rounds the area is considered Electrified. While Electrified, nothing touching the ground may fall Asleep and any Electric-Type Moves deal an additional 3 STAB.

Electrify.description: The next Move the target performs becomes Electric Type, replacing the Move’s other Types.

Electro Ball.description: The target reveals their current Speed stat, applying Combat Stages. If the user’s Speed stat, applying Combat Stages is four times that of the target’s, Electro Ball uses 5d10+16 for Damage Dice Roll; if the user three times that of the target’s, Electro Ball uses 3d10+14; if the user is twice that of the target, Electro Ball uses 2d10+12; if the user is not twice as fast as the target, Electro Ball uses 1d10+10.

Electroweb.description: Electroweb creates a Column 2-meters wide. All Legal Targets are lowered 1 Speed Combat Stage.

Fusion Bolt.description: Fusion Bolt creates a 4-meter Column. If an opponent in this, or the last, round of the encounter used Fusion Flare, Fusion Bolt deals an additional 8d12 in Damage Dice Roll.

Ion Deluge.description: Until the user’s next action, all Normal Types Moves within 10m of the user become Electric Type.

Magnet Rise.description: Magnet Rise grants the user the Levitate Ability until the end of the Encounter. This does not replace the user’s normal Ability. If the user already has Levitate, they now have two instances of Levitate.

Magnetic Flux.description: All allies with the Ability Plus or Minus within 10 meters of the user have their Defense raised 1 Combat Stage and their Special Defense raised 1 Combat Stage.

Parabolic Charge.description: Parabolic Charge creates a 3-meter Burst. After all legal targets take damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target who took the most damage from Parabolic Charge.

Plasma Fists.description: For the remainder of the round, any Normal Type Moves are made into Electric Type Moves.

Shock Wave.description: Shock Wave creates a Column 2-meters wide. Shock Wave cannot miss.

Spark.description: Spark Paralyzes the target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check.

Thunder.description: A 3-meter Blast surrounds the target. Thunder Paralyzes all Legal Targets on 13-20 during Accuracy Check. All Legal Targets within the Blast’s radius are Pushed outward, away from the target, 3 -meters. If the target is in Sunny Weather, Thunder’s Accuracy Check is 11. If the target is in Rainy Weather, Thunder cannot miss. If the target is airborne as a result of Fly or Sky Drop, Thunder cannot miss them

Thunder Fang.description: Thunder Fang Paralyzes or Flinches on 18-19 during Accuracy Check; flip a coin to determine whether the foe gets Paralyzed or Flinches. On 20 during Accuracy Check, the foe is Paralyzed and Flinches.

Thunder Punch.description: Thunder Punch Paralyzes the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Thunder Shock.description: Thunder Shock Paralyzes the target on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Thunder Wave.description: Thunder Wave cannot miss. Thunder Wave Paralyzes the target.

Thunderbolt.description: Thunderbolt creates a Column 1 meter wide. Thunderbolt Paralyzes all Legal Targets on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Volt Switch.description: If Volt Switch successfully hits its target, the user deals damage and then immediately is returned to its Poke Ball in the same turn. A New Pokpmon may immediately be sent out.

Volt Tackle.description: The target is pushed back 5-meters. The target takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6. Volt Tackle Paralyzes the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check. After Volt Tackle deals damage, Recoil.

Wild Charge.description: After Wild Charge deals damage, Recoil.

Zap Cannon.description: A 2-meter Blast surrounds the target, Paralyzing all Legal Targets in the Blast.

Zing Zap.description: Zing Zap Flinches the target on 14-20 during Accuracy Check. Fairy Moves

Aromatic Mist.description: Aromatic Mist raises the target’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Baby-Doll Eyes.description: If a foe wants to declare a Move, you may instead use Baby-Doll Eyes on their turn before they can use their Move. Baby-Doll Eyes lowers the target’s Attack 1 Combat Stage.

Charm.description: Charm lowers the target’s Attack 2 Combat Stages.

Crafty Shield.description: If a legal target is being hit by a Move without a Damage Dice Roll, the user may use Crafty Shield. Crafty Shield prevents the target from being effected by Moves without Damage Dice Rolls.

Dazzling Gleam.description: Dazzling Gleam creates a Column 1 meter wide.

Disarming Voice.description: Disarming Voice creates a 4-meter Burst. Disarming Voice cannot miss.

Draining Kiss.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to 3/4th the damage they dealt to the target.

Fairy Lock.description: Both the user and the target are Trapped.

Fairy Wind.description: Fairy Wind creates a Column 3 meters wide.

Fleur Cannon.description: Fleur Cannon creates a 4-meter Blast. Lower the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages after damage.

Floral Healing.description: The target regains HP equal to half of its full HP. The user may target themselves with Floral Healing. If the area is Meadowed, the target recovers to its full HP instead.

Flower Shield.description: All Grass Type Pokpmon within 5 meters of the user have their Defense raised 1 Combat Stage.

Geomancy.description: Once you declare the use of Geomancy, the user’s turn ends. On its next turn, the user may shift and use the rest of Geomancy’s effect. Raise the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages, raise the user’s Special Defense 2 Combat Stages, and raise the user’s Speed 2 Combat Stages.

Misty Terrain.description: For 5 rounds, the area is considered Misty. While Misty, Pokpmon touching the ground cannot become Burned, Frozen, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Asleep.

Moonblast.description: Moonblast lowers the target’s Special Attack 1 Combat Stage on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Moonlight.description: The user regains HP equal to half of its full HP. If it is Sunny, the user gains 2/3 of its full HP. If it is Rainy, Sand Storming or Hailing the user gains ó of their full HP.

Nature’s Madness.description: The target’s HP is halved. Do not apply weakness or resistance to the HP lost. Do not apply stats to the HP lost. When halving the target’s HP with Nature’s Madness, round up.

Play Rough.description: Play Rough lowers the target’s Attack 1 Combat Stage on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Sweet Kiss.description: Sweet Kiss Confuses the target. Fightng Moves

Arm Thrust.description: Arm Thrust can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Arm Thrust attack on that turn.

Bulk Up.description: Raise the user’s Attack 1 Combat Stage and raise the user’s Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Circle Throw.description: The target is Pushed 40-meters expelling them from the encounter if the terrain allows. They may still rejoin the encounter if they want to. During a trainer battle, the target’s owner must recall the Pushed target and replace them.

Close Combat.description: The user’s Defense is lowered 1 Combat Stage and the user’s Special Defense is lowered 1 Combat Stage.

Counter.description: If the user is being hit by a Move that consults Attack for damage, you may use Counter. If the user survives the hit, the target is dealt damage equal to twice the amount of damage the user received. Do not apply weakness, resistance or immunity. Do not apply stats.

Cross Chop.description: Cross Chop is a Critical Hit on 16-20 during Accuracy Check. Cross Chop Pushes the target 1-meter. On Critical Hit, the target is Pushed 5-meters and the target takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6.

Detect.description: If the user is hit by a Move, instead you are not hit by the Move. You do not take any damage nor are you affected by any of the Move’s effects.

Double Kick.description: Double Kick can hit up to 2 times. If Double Kick misses its first target, you may still use it a second time.

Drain Punch.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target.

Final Gambit.description: Final Gambit lowers the user to 0 HP, and deals 1 point of damage to the foe for every point of health lost this way. Final Gambit does not cause items to activate.

Flying Press.description: When applying weakness and resistance, treat Flying Press as both Fighting and Flying type.

Focus Blast.description: Focus Blast lower the target’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Focus Punch.description: Once you declare the use of Focus Punch the user’s turn ends. If you are not hit by a damaging Move until your next turn you may use Focus Punch’s damage and effects. The target is Pushed 10-meters and it takes an additional 2d12 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d12. If the user is hit by a damaging Move before it’s next turn, it may not use a Move on that turn.

Force Palm.description: Force Palm Paralyzes the target on 1820 during Accuracy Check.

Hammer Arm.description: The user lowers their Speed 1 Combat Stage.

High Jump Kick.description: If High Jump Kick misses, roll 4d10 and subtract the result from the user’s HP. Do not apply weakness, resistance or stats.

Jump Kick.description: If Jump Kick misses, roll 2d10 and subtract the result from the user’s HP. Do not apply weakness, resistance or stats.

Karate Chop.description: Karate Chop is a Critical Hit on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Low Kick.description: If the target is in Weight Class 1, use 1d10 for Damage Dice Roll. If the target is in Weight Class 2, use 1d10+5. If the target is in Weight Class 3, use 1d10+10. If the target is in Weight Class 4, use 2d10+12. If the target is in Weight Class 5, use 3d10+14. If the target is in Weight Class 6, use 5d10+16 for damage.

Low Sweep.description: Lowers the target’s Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Mach Punch.description: If a foe wants to declare a Move, you may instead use Mach Punch on their turn before they can use their Move.

Mat Block.description: Mat Block can only be used on the first turn of an Encounter, or on the first turn the Pokpmon could act after coming out of a Poke Ball. Until the user’s next turn, if any adjacent allies are hit by a Move with a Damage Dice Roll, instead they are not hit by the Move. They do not take any damage nor are they affected by any of the Move’s effects.

Power-Up Punch.description: Power-Up Punch raises the user’s Attack 1 Combat Stage.

Quick Guard.description: Quick Guard prevents the user and allies from being targeted by Moves used as an Interrupt or Intercept for one round. For that round Interrupt or Intercept attacks can only target you, or other allies using Quick Guard.

Revenge.description: If the target damaged Revenge’s user on their last turn, deals 4d12+16 instead during Damage Dice Roll.

Reversal.description: If the user has more than 70% of its total HP, use 1d10+5 for Damage Dice Roll. If the user has more than 36% of its total HP, use 2d10+10. If the user has more than 21% of its total HP, use 3d10+10. If the user has more than 6% of its total HP, use 4d10+10. If the user has 1-5% of its total HP, use 5d10+20.

Rock Smash.description: Rock Smash lowers the target’s Defense 1 Combat Stage on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Rolling Kick.description: Rolling Kick Flinches the target on 1520 during Accuracy Check.

Sacred Sword.description: Sacred Sword’s Accuracy Check may not be modified.

Secret Sword.description: When calculating damage, the target must use their Defense against Secret Sword.

Seismic Toss.description: The target loses HP equal to the level of Seismic Toss’s user. Do not apply weakness or resistance. Do not apply stats.

Sky Uppercut.description: The target is Pushed 2-meters. If the target is airborne, Sky Uppercut deals an additional 2d10. If the target is airborne as a result of Fly, Sky Drop, or a Sky Skill, Sky Uppercut cannot miss as long as you are in a space adjacent to the airborne target.

Storm Throw.description: Storm Throw is a Critical Hit. Do not double Storm Throw’s Damage Dice Roll for Critical Hits.

Superpower.description: Superpower lowers the user’s Attack 1 Combat Stage and Defense by 1 Combat Stage. The target is Pushed 6-meters and it takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6.

Triple Kick.description: Triple Kick can hit up to 3 times. If Triple Kick misses its first or second target, you may still use it a second and third time. For the third use of Triple Kick, if you successfully hit the same target for the first two uses of Triple Kick use 3d12+8 for Triple Kick’s Damage Dice Roll.

Vacuum Wave.description: If a foe wants to declare a Move, you may instead use Vacuum Wave on their turn before they can use their Move.

Vital Throw.description: Vital Throw may only be used if the user was targeted by a Move on a foe’s last turn while the foe was adjacent. Vital Throw can only target that foe. Vital Throw cannot miss. The target is Pushed 5-meters and it takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6.

Wake-Up Slap.description: If the target is Asleep, Wake-Up Slap does an additional 5d10 for Damage Dice Roll and cures the target of Sleep. Fire Moves

Blast Burn.description: Blast Burn creates a 3-meter Blast. On Miss, Blast Burn deals the user’s Special Attack instead to all possible targets.

Blaze Kick.description: Blaze Kick Pushes the target 2-meters. Blaze Kick Burns the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check. Blaze Kick is a Critical Hit on 16-20 during Accuracy Check.

Blue Flare.description: Blue Flare Burns the target on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Burn Up.description: Burn Up creates a 5-meter Burst. The user loses its Fire Type for 1 hour. If the user is only Fire Type, it becomes a Normal Type for 1 hour.

Ember.description: Ember Burns the target on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Eruption.description: Eruption creates a 10-meter Burst. Eruption may not be performed unless the user has at least 90% of its HP.

Fiery Dance.description: Fiery Dance raises the user’s Special Attack by 1 Combat Stage on 11-20 on Accuracy Check.

Fire Blast.description: Fire Blast creates a 2-meter Blast. Fire Blast burns all Legal Targets on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Fire Fang.description: Fire Fang Burns or Flinches on 18-19 during Accuracy Check; flip a coin to determine whether the foe gets Burned or Flinches. On 20 during Accuracy Check, the foe is Burned and Flinches.

Fire Lash.description: Fire Lash lowers the target’s Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Fire Pledge.description: If used on the same turn as Water Pledge, all secondary effects of Moves are activated at a -3 during the Accuracy Check for 1d4+1 turns. If used on the same turn as Grass Pledge, all foes within 20 meters of the user lose 1/8th of their max HP at the end of each round of combat for 1d4+1 turns.

Fire Punch.description: Fire Punch Burns the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Fire Spin.description: Fire Spin Traps the target for 1d4+1 turns. At the beginning of the target’s turn, if the target is Trapped, roll 1d12 and the target loses that much HP. Do not apply weakness or resistance to the HP lost. Do not apply stats to the HP lost. Fire Spin may not trap more than one target at a time.

Flame Burst.description: Flame Burst creates a 4-meter Blast. All Legal Targets, not including the central target, hit in the Blast lose 1/16th of their max HP instead of taking the Damage Dice Roll for damage.

Flame Charge.description: Raise the user’s Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Flame Wheel.description: Flame Wheel Burns the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Flamethrower.description: Flamethrower creates a Column 1 meter wide. Flamethrower Burns the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Flare Blitz.description: The target is pushed back 5-meters. The target takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6. Flare Blitz Burns the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check. After Flare Blitz deals damage, Recoil.

Fusion Flare.description: Fusion Flare creates a 4-meter Column. If an opponent in this, or the last, round of the encounter used Fusion Bolt, Fusion Flare deals an additional 8d12 in Damage Dice Roll.

Heat Crash.description: If the user is in the same Weight Class as the target, use 1d10 for Damage Dice Roll; if the user is one Weight Class higher than the target, use 1d10+10; if the user is two Weight Classes higher than the target, use 2d12+12; if three classes, 3d10+14; if four classes, 5d10+16.

Heat Wave.description: Heat Wave creates a 3-meter burst. Heat Wave Burns all Legal Targets on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Incinerate.description: If the target is holding a Berry, the Berry is destroyed, without its ability activating.

Inferno.description: Inferno creates a 2-meter Blast. Inferno Burns all Legal Targets.

Lava Plume.description: Lava Plume Burns the target on 16-20 during Accuracy Check.

Magma Storm.description: Magma Storm Traps the target for 1d4+1 turns. At the beginning of the target’s turn, if the target is Trapped, roll 2d20 and the target loses that much HP. Do not apply weakness or resistance to the HP lost. Do not apply stats to the HP lost. Magma Storm may not trap more than one target at a time.

Mind Blown.description: Mind Blown creates an 8-meter Burst The user loses half of its current HP.

Mystical Fire.description: Mystical Fire lowers the target’s Special Attack 1 stage.

Overheat.description: Overheat creates a 2-meter Burst. Lower the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages after damage.

Sacred Fire.description: Sacred Fire creates a 7-meter Burst. Sacred Fire Burns all Legal Targets in the Burst on 11-20 during Accuracy Check.

Searing Shot.description: Searing Shot Burns the Target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check.

Shell Trap.description: Set 1 square meters of Shell Trap, “Bomb,” adjacent to the user. If anyone shifts over or onto the Bomb, it deals 7d10+28 Fire Type Damage. Apply Shell Trap’s user’s Special Attack to the damage.

Sunny Day.description: For 1d4+1 rounds, the area is considered Sunny. While Sunny, Fire-Type attacks deal 1.5x damage. Water-Type attacks deal ½ the damage they normally would.

V-Create.description: V-Create creates a 10-meter Burst. Lower the user’s Defense 1 Combat Stage, lower the user’ Special Defense 1 Combat Stage and lower the user’s Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Will-O-Wisp.description: The target is Burned. Flying Moves

Acrobatics.description: If the user is not holding an item, Acrobatics deals 4d12+16 instead during Damage Dice Roll.

Aeroblast.description: Aeroblast creates a Column 2-meters wide. Aeroblast is a Critical Hit on 1120 during Accuracy Check.

Air Cutter.description: Air Cutter is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Air Slash.description: Air Slash Flinches the target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check.

Beak Blast.description: Once you declare the use of Beak Blast, the user’s turn ends. On its next turn, the user may shift and use Beak Blast’s damage and effect. If Beak Blast’s user is attacked with a Melee ranged Move before executing Beak Blast on the user’s second turn, the attacker is Burned.

Bounce.description: Once you declare the use of Bounce, the user is moved up to 10-meters into the air and then the user’s turn ends. On your next turn, shift while in the air, lower your height back to the ground, and then use Bounce’s damage and the rest of its Effect. Bounce creates a Burst equal half the user’s Weight Class. Bounce Paralyzes all Legal Targets on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Brave Bird.description: The target is pushed back 5-meters. The target takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6. After Brave Bird deals damage, Recoil.

Chatter.description: Chatter creates a Column 1 meter wide. Chatter Confuses all Legal Targets.

Defog.description: For 1d4+1 rounds, the area is considered Clear. While Clear, all hindering terrain is no longer hindering terrain and all bonuses or penalties to Accuracy Checks are ignored. All Walls, Coats and Hazards are destroyed.

Feather Dance.description: Feather Dance creates a 1-meter Blast. All Legal Targets in the Blast have their Attack lowered 2 Combat Stages.

Fly.description: Once you declare the use of Fly, the user is shifted 25-meters into the air and then the user’s turn ends. On your next turn, shift while in the air, lower the user back to the target’s level on the battlefield, then use Fly’s damage.

Gust.description: If the target is airborne as a result of Fly or Sky Drop, Gust can hit them, ignoring Range and deals 2d12+16 for its Damage Dice Roll instead.

Hurricane.description: Hurricane creates a 4-meter Blast. Hurricane Confuses all Legal Targets on 13 -20 during Accuracy Check. All Legal Targets within the Blast’s radius are Pushed outward, away from the target, 3 -meters. If the target is in Sunny Weather, Hurricane’s Accuracy Check is 11. If the target is in Rainy Weather, Hurricane cannot miss. If the target is airborne as a result of Fly or Sky Drop, Hurricane cannot miss them.

Mirror Move.description: Use the Move the target has used on their last turn. You may choose new targets for the Move. Mirror Move cannot miss.

Oblivion Wing.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to 3/4th the damage they dealt to the target.

Pluck.description: If the target is holding a Berry, Pluck deals an additional 2d10 during Damage Dice Roll and Pluck destroys the Berry Then Pluck’s user uses the Berry’s effect.

Roost.description: The user regains HP equal to half of its full HP. The user loses any resistances or immunities to Ground-type Moves until the end of their next turn.

Sky Attack.description: Once you declare the use of Sky Attack, the user is moved 10-meters into the air and then the user’s turn ends. On your next turn, shift while in the air, lower your height back to the target and then use Sky Attack’s damage. Sky Attack Flinches the target on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Sky Drop.description: Once you declare the use of Sky Drop, the user and target are moved 25-meters into the air, the Target is Trapped and then the user’s turn ends. On your next turn, shift while in the air, lower both the user and the target’s heights back to the ground, then use Sky Drop’s damage and the target is no longer Trapped. This move does not damage Flying type Pokpmon. If used without following up with the rest of Sky Drop’s actions on the turn following its use, Lower the user and the target back down, do not use Sky Drop’s damage and the target is no longer Trapped.

Tailwind.description: Tailwind creates a Column 5-meters wide. For 1d6+1 turns, Tailwind’s user and all of the user’s allies in the Column have their Speed raised 1 Combat Stage. When the user or the user’s allies leave the Column or when the Column disappears, their Speed is lowered 1 Combat Stage. Whenever you make a new Tailwind Column, the last one the user made disappears.

Astonish.description: Astonish Flinches the target on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Confuse Ray.description: Confuse Ray creates a Column 1 meter wide. Confuse Ray cannot miss. All targets are Confused.

Curse.description: The user loses ½ of their full HP and the target Pokpmon is Cursed. A Cursed Pokpmon loses 1/8th of their full HP at the beginning of each of their turns. Do not apply weakness, resistance or stats to the lost HP. The user’s Speed is lowered 1 Combat Stage. The user’s Attack is raised 1 Combat Stage and the user’s Defense is raised 1 Combat Stage.

Destiny Bond.description: When the user faints after taking damage from a Move, you may use Destiny Bond as an Intercept to target the Pokpmon who lowered the user to 0 HP or less. Roll 1d20, on a roll of 6 or better the target is set to 0 HP.

Grudge.description: When the user faints, you may use Grudge as an Intercept. The Move that caused the user to drop to 0 HP or less has its Frequency changed to Center for 1 hour.

Hex.description: If the target has a status affliction, Hex deals 4d12+16 instead during Damage Dice Roll.

Lick.description: Lick Paralyzes the target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check.

Moongeist Beam.description: Moongeist Beam creates a 3-meter Blast. All Legal Targets may not use Abilities or Moves to negate Moongeist Beam.

Night Shade.description: The target loses HP equal to the level of Night Shade’s user. Do not apply weakness or resistance. Do not apply stats.

Nightmare.description: Nightmare can only hit Legal Targets that are Asleep. Nightmare gives a special affliction, Bad Sleep. Bad Sleep makes a target lose ó of its full HP per turn while it is Bad Sleeping. When the target wakes up, they are cured of Bad Sleep.

Ominous Wind.description: Ominous Wind creates a Column that is 2-meters wide. If you rolled 11-20 during Accuracy Check for Ominous Wind, the user has each of its stats raised 1 Combat Stage. If you rolled 16-20 on the Accuracy Roll, all allies in the Column also have each of their stats raised 1 Combat Stage and are not damaged by Ominous Wind.

Phantom Force.description: Once you declare the use of Phantom Force, the user is removed from the field and the user’s turn ends. On the next turn, Phantom Force’s user appears adjacent to any target, ignoring Speed Skills, and then uses Phantom Force’s damage.

Shadow Ball.description: Shadow Ball lowers the foe’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Shadow Bone.description: Shadow Bone lowers the target’s Defense 1 Combat Stage on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Shadow Claw.description: Shadow Claw is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Shadow Force.description: Once you declare the use of Shadow Force, the user is removed from the field and the user’s turn ends. On the next turn, Shadow Force’s user appears adjacent to any target, ignoring Speed Skills, and then uses Shadow Force’s damage.

Shadow Sneak.description: If a foe wants to declare a Move, you may instead use Shadow Sneak on their turn before they can use their Move.

Spectral Thief.description: Set the target’s Combat Stages back to 0, then add any positive Combat Stages that the target had, to Spectral Thief’s user before damage. Grass Moves

Spirit Shackle.description: On hit, the target may not flee or be switched until it is knocked out, for the remainder of the encounter.

Spite.description: Spite lowers the target’s last move’s Frequency for the remainder of the encounter. EOT Frequency Moves are changed into Battle Frequency. At-Will Frequency Moves are changed to EOT Frequency. Spite may not target the same target’s Move more than once per encounter.

Trick-or-Treat.description: For 1d4+1 turns, the target gains the Ghost Type if it isn’t already, in addition to its other type(s). The target can have up to 3 types as a result of Trick-or

Absorb.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target.

Aromatherapy.description: The target has 1 status effect of their choice removed. Aromatherapy cannot miss.

Bullet Seed.description: Bullet Seed can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Bullet Seed attack on that turn.

Cotton Guard.description: Raise the user’s Defense 3 Combat Stages.

Cotton Spore.description: Cotton Spore creates a 2-meter Blast. All Legal Targets have their Speed lowered 2 Combat Stages.

Energy Ball.description: Energy Ball lowers the foe’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Forest’s Curse.description: For 1d4+1 turns, the target gains the Grass Type if it isn’t already, in addition to its other type(s). The target can have up to 3 types as a result of Forest’s

Frenzy Plant.description: Frenzy Plant creates a 3-meter Blast. On Miss, Frenzy Plant deals the user’s Special Attack instead to all possible targets.

Giga Drain.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target.

Grass Knot.description: If the target is in Weight Class 1, use 1d10 for Damage Dice Roll. If the target is in Weight Class 2, use 1d10+5. If the target is in Weight Class 3, use 1d10+10. If the target is in Weight Class 4, use 2d10+12. If the target is in Weight Class 5, use 3d10+14. If the target is in Weight Class 6, use 5d10+16 for damage.

Grass Pledge.description: If used on the same turn as Water Pledge, all foes are treated as if at -2 Speed Combat Stages for 1d4+1 turns, if any foe has additional Speed Combat Stages, apply them as usual. If used on the same turn as Fire Pledge, all foes within 20 meters of the user lose 1/8th of their max HP at the end of each round of combat for 1d4+1 turns.

Grass Whistle.description: Grass Whistle creates a 5-meter Burst. All Legal Targets fall Asleep.

Grassy Terrain.description: For 5 rounds, the area is considered Meadowed. While Meadowed, Grass Moves with Damage Dice Rolls deal an additional 2 STAB and at the end of any Grass Type Pokpmon’s turn where they acted, they recover 1/16th of their full HP.

Horn Leech.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target.

Ingrain.description: Ingrain is a Coat with the following "abilities": The user cannot be forced to switch out or flee but they can still be willingly switched out. At the beginning of each of the user’s turns, the user gains 1/16th of its full HP. The user loses any immunities to Ground-Type Moves.

Leaf Blade.description: Leaf Blade is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Leaf Storm.description: Leaf Storm creates a 2-meter Column. Lower the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages after damage.

Leaf Tornado.description: Leaf Tornado creates a 2-meter Burst. On 15-20 during Accuracy Check, all Legal Targets must roll 1 higher on Accuracy Checks for the remainder of the combat.

Leech Seed.description: At the beginning of each of the target’s turns, Leech Seed’s target loses 1/8th of their full HP. Do not apply weakness or resistance to the HP lost. Do not apply stats to the HP lost. Leech Seed’s user then gains HP equal to the amount the target lost. Leech Seed lasts until the target faints or is returned to a Poke Ball. Leech Seed cannot hit a Grass type Pokpmon.

Mega Drain.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target.

Needle Arm.description: Needle Arm Flinches the target on 1520 during Accuracy Check.

Petal Dance.description: Petal Dance’s user must make another Ranged attack for 1d2 more turns. The attack is identical to Petal Dance except for Petal Dance’s effect. Petal Dance creates a 3-meter Burst, but only on the first round of its use. After the first use is declared, Petal Dance targets the nearest Legal Target for its second or third round of use if the original target is unconscious. If potential targets are an equal distance from the user, you may choose the target. After Petal Dance’s additional uses ends, the user becomes Confused. For Pokpmon users, the additional 1d2 are their Trainer’s Command Actions, unless the Trainer is unconscious, in which case the Pokpmon acts on its own until finished.

Razor Leaf.description: Razor Leaf is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Seed Flare.description: Seed Flare creates a 3-meter Blast. All Legal Targets have their Special Defense lowered 1 Combat Stage.

Sleep Powder.description: Sleep Powder creates a 1-meter Blast. All Legal Targets fall Asleep.

Solar Beam.description: Once you declare the use of Solar Beam, the user’s turn ends. On its next turn, the user may shift and use Solar Beam’s damage. If the user is in Sunny Weather, Solar Beam uses its damage on the turn you declare its use. If the user is in Rainy or Sand Storming Weather Solar Beam’s user must end their turn immediately at the start of their second turn after declaring Solar Beam’s use, only getting to use Solar Beam’s damage on the third turn. If the user is in Hailing Weather, Solar Beam’s damage is 3d10. Solar Beam creates a Column 1 meter wide.

Solar Blade.description: Once you declare the use of Solar Blade, the user’s turn ends. On its next turn, the user may shift three times and use Solar Blade’s damage. If the user is in Sunny Weather, Solar Blade uses its damage on the turn you declare its use. If the user is in Rainy or Sand Storming Weather Solar Blade’s user must end their turn immediately at the start of their second turn after declaring Solar Blade’s use, only getting to use Solar Blade’s damage on the third turn. If the user is in Hailing Weather, Solar Blade’s damage is 3d10.

Spiky Shield.description: If the user is hit by a Move, instead you are not hit by the Move. You do not take any damage nor are you affected by any of the Move’s effects. If the Move was Melee ranged, the attacker loses 1/8th of their full HP. Do not apply weakness or resistance when losing HP this way.

Strength Sap.description: The user regains HP equal to the target’s current Attack. Then, the target’s Attack is lowered 1 Combat Stage.

Stun Spore.description: Stun Spore creates a 1-meter Blast. Stun Spore paralyzes al Legal Targets.

Synthesis.description: The user regains HP equal to half of its full HP. If it is Sunny, the user gains 2/3 of its full HP. If it is Rainy, Sand Storming or Hailing the user gains ó of their full HP.

Trop Kick.description: Trop Kick lowers the target’s Attack 1 Combat Stage.

Wood Hammer.description: The target is pushed back 5-meters. The target takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6. After Wood Hammer deals damage, Recoil.

Worry Seed.description: The target’s Ability is replaced with Insomnia. If the target has multiple Abilities, Worry Seed only replaces one. Ground Moves

Bone Club.description: Bone Club Flinches the target on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Bone Rush.description: Bone Rush can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Bone Rush attack on that turn.

Bonemerang.description: Bonemerang can hit up to 2 times. If Bonemerang misses its first target, you may still use it a second time.

Bulldoze.description: Bulldoze creates a 3-meter Burst. All Legal Targets are lowered 1 Speed Combat Stage.

Dig.description: Once you declare the use of Dig, the user is moved underground and their turn ends. On the next turn, Dig’s user may shift, using their Overland or Burrow Speed then uses Dig’s damage. While underground, Dig’s user may not be the target of Moves.

Drill Run.description: Drill Run is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Earth Power.description: Earth Power creates a 3-meter Blast. Earth Power lowers the Special Defense of all Legal Targets 1 Combat Stage on 16-20 during Accuracy Check.

Earthquake.description: Earthquake creates a 5-meter Burst. Earthquake can hit targets using the Move Dig or using a Burrow Skill.

Fissure.description: Fissure creates a Column that is 3meters wide. All Legal Targets have their HP set to 0. Fissure can hit targets using the Move Dig or using a Burrow Skill.

Magnitude.description: Magnitude creates a 2-meter Burst. Roll 1d6. On a result of 1, use 1d4 for damage. On a result of 2, use 1d8 for damage. On a result of 3, use 2d8 for damage. On a result of 4, use 2d10 for damage. On a result of 5, use 3d10 for damage. On a result of 6, use 4d10 for damage. Magnitude can hit targets using the Move Dig or a Burrow Skill.

Mud Bomb.description: Mud Bomb creates a 1-meter Blast. All Legal Targets must roll +1 during Accuracy Checks for the remainder of the encounter on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Mud Shot.description: Mud Shot creates a Column 1 meter wide. All Legal Targets have their Speed lowered 1 Combat Stage.

Mud Sport.description: Mud Sport makes a Coat that resists Electric Moves. Mud Sport can be used to Target Self.

Mud-Slap.description: Mud-Slap creates a Column 1-meter wide. All Legal Targets must roll +1 during Accuracy Checks for the remainder of the encounter.

Rototiller.description: All Grass Type allies within 10 meters of the user have their Attack raised 1 Combat Stage and their Special Attack raised 1 Combat Stage.

Sand Tomb.description: Sand Tomb Traps the target for 1d4+1 turns. At the beginning of the target’s turn, if the target is Trapped, roll 1d20 and the target loses that much HP. Do not apply weakness or resistance to the HP lost. Do not apply stats to the HP lost. Sand Tomb may not trap more than one target at a time.

Sand-Attack.description: Sand-Attack creates a Column 1 meter wide. All Legal Targets must roll +1 during Accuracy Checks for the remainder of the encounter.

Shore Up.description: The user regains HP equal to half of its full HP. If it is Sandstorming, the user gains 2/3 of its full HP. Ice Moves

Spikes.description: Set 10 square meters of Spikes, all 10 meters must be adjacent with at least one other space of Spikes next to each other. Spikes cause all foes to lose 1/8th of their full HP when moving onto or over the occupied spaces with an Overland Skill. If there are 2 Layers of Spikes on the same space, the foes lose 1/6th of their full HP. If there are 3 or more Layers of Spikes on the same space, the foes lose 1/4th of their full HP.

Stomping Tantrum.description: Stomping Tantrum creates a 5-meter Burst. If Stomping Tantrum’s user missed its last attack, or failed to successfully execute a Move during its last turn, use 6d12+22 for damage instead.

Aurora Beam.description: Aurora Beam creates a Column 1 meter wide. Aurora Beam lowers the target’s Attack 1 Combat Stage on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Aurora Veil.description: The target with Aurora Veil’s Coat reduces all damage from attacks by half as long as the target with Aurora Veil’s Coat is in Hailing Weather. Aurora Veil can be used to Target Self.

Avalanche.description: Avalanche creates a Column 2-meters wide. If a target damaged Avalanche’s user on their last turn, deal an additional 2d10 during Damage Dice Roll.

Blizzard.description: Blizzard creates a Column that is 3meters wide. Blizzard Freezes all legal targets on 16-20 during accuracy Check. If the target is in Hailing Weather, Blizzard cannot miss.

Freeze Shock.description: Freeze Shock creates a Column 2meters wide. All Legal Targets are pushed 10-meters and take an additional 1d6 if Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6. Freeze Shock paralyzes all Legal Targets on 15 -20 on Accuracy Check.

Freeze-Dry.description: Freeze-Dry Freezes the Target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check. When applying weakness and resistance, Freeze-Dry is always super-effective on Water-type Pokpmon.

Frost Breath.description: Frost Breath is a Critical Hit. Do not double Frost Breath’s Damage Dice Roll for Critical Hits.

Glaciate.description: Glaciate creates a 5-meter Burst. All Legal Targets have their Speed lowered 1 Combat Stage. On a roll of 11-20 during Accuracy Check, all Legal Targets on the ground are Trapped and cannot shift for 2 rounds.

Hail.description: For 1d4+1 rounds, the area is considered Hailing. At the beginning of each round, all non-Ice Type Pokpmon lose 1/16th of their full HP. Ice Type Pokpmon are treated as if their Defense is raised 2 Combat Stages while Hailing.

Haze.description: For 3 rounds, the area is considered Hazy. Hazy does not replace other Weather Statuses. In Hazy Weather, all Combat Stages get set to 0.

Ice Ball.description: The user must use Ice Ball until it misses, or fails to be able to shift enough to hit a target for their next 4 turns. On the second turn, Ice Ball uses 2d10+8 for its Damage Dice Roll. On the third turn, Ice Ball uses 2d10+12. On the fourth turn, Ice Ball uses 3d10+16. On the fifth and final turn, Ice Ball uses 4d10+20.

Ice Beam.description: Ice Beam creates a Column 1 meter wide. Ice Beam Freezes all Legal Targets on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Ice Burn.description: Ice Burn creates a Column 2-meters wide. All Legal Targets are pushed 10meters and take an additional 1d6 if Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6. Ice Burn Burns all Legal Targets on 15-20 on Accuracy Check.

Ice Fang.description: Ice Fang Freezes or Flinches on 18-19 during Accuracy Check; flip a coin to determine whether the foe gets Frozen or Flinches. On 20 during Accuracy Check, the foe is Frozen and Flinches.

Ice Hammer.description: The user lowers their Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Ice Punch.description: Ice Punch Freezes the target on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Ice Shard.description: If a foe wants to declare a Move, you may instead use Ice Shard on their turn before they can use their Move.

Icicle Crash.description: Icicle Crash Flinches the target on 1520 during Accuracy Check.

Icicle Spear.description: Icicle Spear can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Icicle Spear attack on that turn.

Icy Wind.description: Icy Wind creates a Column 1-meter wide. All Legal Targets have their Speed lowered 1 Combat Stage. Normal Moves

Mist.description: The target with Mist’s Coat may not have their Combat Stages lowered. Mist can Target Self.

Powder Snow.description: Powder Snow creates a 1-meter wide. Powder Snow Freezes all Legal Targets on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Sheer Cold.description: Sheer Cold creates 3-meter Blast. All Legal Targets have their HP set to 0.

Acupressure.description: Roll 1d6. On a result of 1, raise the target’s Attack 2 Combat Stages. On a result of 2, raise the target’s Defense 2 Combat Stages. On a result of 3, raise the target’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages. On a result of 4, raise the target’s Special Defense 2 Combat Stages. On a result of 5, raise the target’s Speed 2 Combat Stages. On a result of 6, the target needs -2 to hit during Accuracy Checks.

After You.description: The target goes first during the next round of combat, ignoring Speed stats. They may not be Interrupted by any Moves that round.

Assist.description: Randomly select another Pokpmon on the user’s roster and then randomly select a Move that Pokpmon knows. Assist’s user uses that Move immediately.

Attract.description: Attract Infatuates the target. Attract may not affect something that is the same gender as the user or something that is genderless.

Barrage.description: Barrage can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Barrage attack on that turn.

Baton Pass.description: The user is replaced with another Pokpmon from their trainer’s roster. All Combat Stages and Coats on Baton Pass’ user are transferred to the replacement. Baton Pass can ignore Traps, but the replacement is then Trapped.

Belly Drum.description: Belly Drum’s user loses HP equal to half of its full HP. Do not apply the user’s stats to the HP lost. Belly Drum sets the user’s Attack to +6 Combat Stages.

Bestow.description: The user gives its held item to the target, unless the target is already holding an item.

Bide.description: You may use Bide as an Intercept upon being targeted by a Move. After declaring the use of Bide, the user ends their turn. At the start of their next turn, end their turn. On the turn after that, add up all of the damage the user has taken in the past 2 turns, call that total X. Bide’s Target loses HP equal to twice the value of X. Do not apply weakness or resistance to the HP lost as a result of Bide. Do not apply stats to the HP lost as a result of Bide. Bide cannot miss.

Bind.description: Bind Traps the target and the user for 1d4+1 turns. At the beginning of the target’s turn, if the target is Trapped, roll 1d12 and the target loses that much HP. Do not apply weakness or resistance to the HP lost. Do not apply stats to the HP lost. Bind may not trap more than one target at a time unless the User is Huge or Gigantic.

Block.description: Both the user and the target are Trapped.

Body Slam.description: Body Slam Paralyzes the target on 1520 during Accuracy Check.

Camouflage.description: The user changes their Type to match the field. Forests and grassy areas change the user into Grass-Type. Watery areas change the user into WaterType. Caves and Mountains could change the user into Rock-Type or Ground-Type. An icy terrain would turn the user into IceType. A building may change the user into Steel-Type or Normal-Type. Weather affects what Type the user becomes. If you are having difficultly determining what type the user should become, consult the GM.

Captivate.description: Captivate lowers the target’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages. Captivate may not affect something that is the same gender as the user or something that is genderless.

Chip Away.description: Ignore any changes in the target’s Defense or Special Defense when calculating damage.

Comet Punch.description: Comet Punch can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Comet Punch attack on that turn. When adding stats only add ½ Attack.

Confide.description: The target’s Special Attack is lowered 1 Combat Stage.

Constrict.description: Constrict lowers the target’s Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Conversion.description: The user becomes the elemental Type of their choice as long as they have a Move that is the same elemental Type until the end of the encounter. Replace all other Types.

Conversion2.description: The user becomes the elemental Type of their choice as long as the Type resists the elemental Type of the Move it last took damage from until the end of the encounter. Replace all other Types.

Copycat.description: Use the Move the target has used on their last turn. You may choose new targets for the Move. Copycat cannot miss.

Covet.description: Covet takes the target’s held item and attaches it to Covet’s user, if the user is not holding anything.

Crush Claw.description: 3d10+12 EOT - 3 Crush Claw lowers the target’s Defense 1 Combat Stage on 11-20 during Accuracy Check.

Crush Grip.description: For every 10% the target is below their full HP, Crush Grip’s Damage Dice Roll is reduced by 1d12 to a minimum of 20.

Defense Curl.description: Defense Curl raises the user’s Defense 1 Combat Stage. If the user uses the Move Ice Ball or Rollout later in the encounter without having been switched out, each hit deals an additional 1d12+4 during Damage Dice Roll. The extra damage bonus for Ice Ball or Rollout does not stack.
